---
title: "Communications"
---

::: {.page-lead}
Awards, talks, media coverage, and the training and mentoring behind the research.
:::

[Awards]{.eyebrow .lg}

- **Best Oral Presentation Award**, DUNES Symposium, Singapore, 2025
- **Best Poster Award**, SfN Singapore Chapter Annual Meeting, Singapore, 2024
- **2nd Place Poster Award**, Northwest Developmental Biology Conference, Seattle, 2018

I've also presented my optogenetics work at international meetings including 2024 NeuroFly
(Birmingham, UK), the 2024 Asia-Pacific Drosophila Neurobiology Conference (Tokyo, Japan), and
the 2025 Francis Crick Symposium (Suzhou, China).

[]{.section-rule}

[Courses & training]{.eyebrow .lg}

:::: {.stack}

::: {.scard}
[Chen Institute–Duke–NUS AI Boot Camp](https://www.cheninstitute.org/events-new/duke-bootcamp){.scard-title}
[Duke-NUS · June 2026]{.scard-meta}

A selective four-day intensive run by Duke-NUS with Duke University's Department of Computer
Science. It covered the fundamentals of modern AI: how large language models and foundation
models work, designing and evaluating AI workflows, the strengths and limits of current
models, and the safety and responsible-use questions that come with them. I attended to build
a solid working foundation in these tools and to bring them to bear on high-throughput
behavioral data.
:::

::::

[]{.section-rule}

[Media]{.eyebrow .lg}

:::: {.stack}

::: {.scard}
[Duke-NUS researchers develop light-controlled "switch" for brain cells to better study the brain](https://www.straitstimes.com/singapore/duke-nus-researchers-develop-light-controlled-switch-for-brain-cells-to-better-study-the-brain){.scard-title}
[The Straits Times · 2024]{.scard-meta}

Media coverage of our *Nature Communications* study introducing kalium channelrhodopsins
(KCRs), a light-controlled "off switch" that silences neurons with greater selectivity than
existing tools. I led the *C. elegans* work that validated the approach across species.

[Read the article →](https://www.straitstimes.com/singapore/duke-nus-researchers-develop-light-controlled-switch-for-brain-cells-to-better-study-the-brain){.read-more}
:::

::: {.scard}
[DUNES Symposium 2025](https://www.duke-nus.edu.sg/newshub/our-stories/dunes-symposium-2025){.scard-title}
[Duke-NUS Newshub · 2025]{.scard-meta}

Duke-NUS's feature on the 17th DUNES Symposium and its early-career researchers. My oral
presentation on optogenetic control of valence and locomotion in *Drosophila* was awarded Best
Oral Presentation.

[Read the feature →](https://www.duke-nus.edu.sg/newshub/our-stories/dunes-symposium-2025){.read-more}
:::

::::

[]{.section-rule}

[Mentoring]{.eyebrow .lg}

I've mentored three undergraduate student researchers. In 2024 at Duke-NUS, I guided a Yale-NUS
College undergraduate on the OPN3 optogenetic-silencing project, through experimental design, data
collection, and analysis; their contributions fed into the study, and they later joined the lab as
a full-time research assistant. From 2016 to 2018, I mentored two undergraduate researchers in the
Cabernard lab as they carried out their own student-research projects.
